water.name: Jarbidge River, East Fork and West Fork and tributary streams

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons and Regulations CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Jarbidge River, East Fork and West Fork and tributary streams: Open year around. Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, except possession of bull trout is prohibited. All captured bull trout must be released immediately. Possession: 10 trout and 10 mountain whitefish. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Jarbidge River, East Fork — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Trout species occurring here include native bull trout, redband trout, and mountain whitefish.

sources.title: Jarbidge River, West Fork — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Trout species occurring here include native bull trout, redband trout, and mountain whitefish.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.humanSummary: The East and West Forks of the Jarbidge River and their tributary streams are open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: Daily limit is 5 trout and 5 mountain whitefish, counted separately by species (not combined). Possession limit is 10 trout and 10 mountain whitefish.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit of 5 trout and 5 mountain whitefish (separate limits); possession limit of 10 trout and 10 mountain whitefish.
regulations.verbatimText: Limits — Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish. Possession: 10 trout and 10 mountain whitefish.

parameters.counting_rule: Possession of bull trout is prohibited under any circumstance; bull trout do not count toward, and may not be retained within, the trout limit.

regulations.humanSummary: Possession of bull trout is prohibited on the Jarbidge River forks and tributaries.
regulations.verbatimText: except possession of bull trout is prohibited

parameters.note: Any bull trout caught must be released immediately back into the water; it may not be kept, held, or transported.

regulations.humanSummary: All captured bull trout must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: All captured bull trout must be released immediately.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
